text:
My problem with Spotify is that I literally cannot choose a specific song or album
I tried to listen to a song by the artist Lemon Demon, but when I clicked on the song, it sent me to one of his other songs
After that I tried to favorite the song and listen to it on my favorites playlist, but when I started it it played a song that wasn't even on the playlist
Algorithms have ruined everything…but you hit the nail on the head here. I don’t listen to any Spotify “made for you” playlists anymore because it’s all the same stuff

Imo, Spotify is getting greaddier with their premium feature like I can't skip a song for 1 hour bcuz i can only get 6, i can't press songs many times bcuz it will just play a different one, I  always stuck in shuffle mode and more. 





Heck even Spotify had every Spotify mods turned off bcuz it had too much features if premium, look i get it u want to promote ur premium feature but DON'T OVER- PROMOTE IT will get the free users so annoyed that i had to used another app just to have a peaceful time to listen to my music.
Glad i left Spotify

what I HATE is that my playlists will serve me up some good song from a band I like, like REM, a song I may have never heard. But then it will just keep playing that ONE REM song every time it plays REM. Great job finding a song I've never heard from a band I like, but now I'm friggin sick of that song.

im skeptical to get premium if banner ads of artists i dont even give a fck still appear whenever i left the app on my laptop open for the album cover to set the ambience, then no, what a waste of money, id rather return in my old habit, an adventurous pirate!
Hey Brian, you actually hit the nail on the head here. Algorithms suck at recommending music. I am curious if you have any ideas you'd love to see on a social music platform?
Too many ads is my problem with them 🤷🏻‍♂️

They don't even have High Fidelity audio. THIS is one of the main issues. It's basically like listening to music in 720p as opposed to 4K like in Tidal. I recently made the switch after over 12 years on Spotify and the difference in quality is actually shocking me. They cheap out on data by compressing music despite people paying premiums.
For Android u can't even delete a song from the queue anymore! What a nightmare!!
Apart from it being shitty its actually unusable in numerous occasions in gonna seriously think about prolonging my subscription

I use Apple Music because of how confusing Spotify on phone like when I search up a song it picks a song you didn’t pick or if you want you listen to your playlist it’ll play a song not it the app wants to control what you want to listen to instead of you controlling and listening to the music you like

In trying to use Spotify just now, I remember why I stopped using it. A bullshit interface, seventy million login prompts, technical issues, and for what- I'd rather just blast what I like from my phone filled with my tunes from my computer without any of the bullshit Spotify seems to fetishize.

The playlists now a days a filled with bots/AI that have different accounts so it doesn't look sus.
Why: cause Spotify makes more money that way, to say it simple.
Their focus on AI slop made me switch to youtube
It has the same crap on repeat recommendations, and my workouts are not being fueled by my expectations of what music should be from an app. Time to make limewire 2.0 lol
Spotify here... yeah, We're not listening.
Spotify has always sucked youre paying money for a non premium experience when tidal or apple music exist with hifi quality and paying artists more than pennies
Also because Bad Bunny is pushing with money his stupid music there too. Dirty money pushing dirty music. His stupid influences.
Spotify stinks. YouTube stinks even more. They're both unusable.
Yeah right

Why Spotify sucks... How not to.

Isn’t the real solution simple?
Instead of paying artists based on total global streams, what if my monthly subscription was divided among the artists I actually listened to?

Let’s say my plan is $9.99. Spotify takes 10% ($0.99) as their platform fee — fair enough. That leaves $9.00 to be distributed across the artists I played that month.

Personally, I don’t listen to hundreds of artists. Maybe 30 to 50 in a month — and usually the same ones on repeat. So if I listened to 50 artists, each one would get around $0.18 from my listening habits alone.

Now imagine that artist has 10,000 fans doing the same — that’s $1,800 a month for their work.
That feels fair. That feels human.

Because let’s be honest: we don’t “own” the music we listen to anymore. We’re renting experiences, and our money should follow the artists who create them — not just an algorithm counting plays.

Spotify could still thrive under this model. They’d still keep their cut, and they could continue growing through ad revenue — artist promotion tools, audio/video ads, sponsorships, and other creative formats for non-premium users.

The Big Picture (Estimated Global Impact)

Let’s run the numbers with today’s Spotify stats:
	•	600 million total users
	•	250 million premium subscribers paying $9.99/month
	•	Spotify keeps 10%, giving artists the other 90%

That means:
	•	$2.5 billion/month in total premium revenue
	•	$249.75 million/month for Spotify’s 10% cut
	•	$2.25 billion/month goes directly to artists based on actual listeners

Now add:
	•	10 million artists running $100/month ad campaigns → $1 billion/month in extra revenue for Spotify
	•	350 million free users listening to brand ads at $0.40 CPM (cost per listener):
	•	If each user hears 10 brand ads per month → $1.4 billion/month

Spotify’s total monthly revenue under this model:
$249.75M (subscriptions) + $1B (artist ads) + $1.4B (brand ads) = ≈ $2.65 billion per month

That’s ≈ $31.8 billion per year, while artists get 90% of the subscription pool — a massive win for both sides.

It’s not about breaking Spotify’s business model —
It’s about evolving it.
Fairness and profit can coexist when value flows where it’s earned: from listeners, to the artists they love.

PS: With my calculation Spotify would double their annual income... and I didn't even use the full amount of artists paying ads + all the brands that could run ads as it was with radio.

I've NEVER liked a single Spotify playlist in like 15 years. I forget when I first got Spotify but it was pretty close to the beginning. Unlike many of the chuds in the comments here I have actually been PAYING for Spotify for 15 years. If you're not even on Premium gtfo with your take bc yea obv it's going to be an inferior experience.  What we're talking about here today in this vid is about the piss-poor quality of the algorithm and the playlists. They're generic and bland and suggest the same music over and over. It was okay for finding new artists at first but that ended a long time ago. Being just like old iTunes is what made it. Now it's SLOP.
My playlist keep changing to what I listen to. It’s frustrating because I can’t find new music that way. Even the playlist that someone else created change based on what I listen to.
